title: Bridge Essay: From rubber hands to neuroprosthetics: Neural correlates of embodiment.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through From, rubber, hands, to, neuroprosthetics:.

**From rubber hands to neuroprosthetics: Neural correlates of embodiment.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev. PMID: 37544389. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2023.105351. Our interaction with the world rests on the knowledge that we are abody in space and time, which can interact with the environment. This awareness is usually referred to as sense of embodiment. For the good part of the past 30 years, the rubber hand illusion (RHI) has been aprime tool to study embodiment in healthy and people with avariety of clinical conditions. In this paper, we provide acritical overview of this research with afocus on the RHI paradigm as atool to study prothesis embodi
